nodes.label: AI as helper for complicated tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI for simple or familiar tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI for programming tasks that are too complicated for me to easily do myself

nodes.label: AI as one-shot isolated tool
nodes.grounding_span: I have a strong preference for using AI once for an isolated problem and not for follow up revisions

nodes.label: AI reliability in maintaining context across turns
nodes.label_negative: AI forgetting previous instructions or introducing new issues
nodes.grounding_span: it will often forget syntax instructions I've previously given it, or it will meet the requirement it missed the first time but introduce a different issue

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner that gets close to solution
nodes.grounding_span: Even though the model wasn't able to provide a direct solution it got me close enough to work out the solution myself

nodes.label: Maintaining full understanding of project structure
nodes.label_negative: Extensive AI use disrupting project understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I want to keep a full understanding of the project structure and using AI extensively can disrupt that

nodes.label: Efficiency and time saving
nodes.grounding_span: it often takes more time than fixing the code myself

nodes.label: Personal competence and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I am glad that I get experience implementing AI in game development

nodes.label: Understanding and control over codebase
nodes.grounding_span: I want to keep a full understanding of the project structure

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as collaborative reference
nodes.grounding_span: I felt intense satisfaction... a great example of collaboration with AI

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's inability to maintain context
nodes.grounding_span: trying to get AI to fix its own code can be very frustrating

nodes.label: Openness to expanded collaboration if AI improves
nodes.grounding_span: I would absolutely be open to collaborating more expansively with the models

nodes.label: Preference for handling simple tasks independently
nodes.grounding_span: Anything that's simple or familiar enough to do myself I will typically do it myself

nodes.label: Test-first then self-revise approach
nodes.grounding_span: I will take the function and test it first, identify any refinements that are needed and then usually make the revisions myself

nodes.label: Cross-referencing AI output against documentation
nodes.grounding_span: by cross referencing the AI output against the documentation I was able to figure out
